description: Behavior cases for cross-team dependency negotiation: two-way mapping, early surfacing, concrete commitments, de-risking, accounting for competing priorities, and pre-agreed escalation.

cases.prompt: Our launch depends on three other teams delivering pieces. Plan and negotiate those dependencies.

assertions: Maps dependencies both ways (what we need and what we owe) and marks the critical-path ones.
Turns each into a concrete commitment: deliverable, interface/contract, date, and owner on both sides, written down.
De-risks critical dependencies (fallback, decoupling via stub/flag, parallel paths, SPOFs named).
Accounts for the other teams' priorities and sets a visible status plus a pre-agreed escalation trigger.
Leaves the interface contract to api-event-architect and the integration boundary to architecture-designer.

cases.prompt: The platform team said they'll 'help us out when they can'. That's all we have. Firm it up.

assertions: Refuses to treat 'help when they can' as a plan: no deliverable, date, or owner.
Negotiates a specific deliverable against a defined interface, a date, and a named owner both ways, written down.
Adds a fallback in case it slips and a status/escalation trigger.
Surfaces the dependency early rather than at integration time.

cases.prompt: We're blocked because we can't start until another team finishes their API. Is there any way to not be fully blocked?

assertions: Proposes decoupling: build against the agreed contract with a stub/mock, feature-flag the integration off, and parallelize.
Turns the hard blocker into a soft one so a slip is a setback, not a stop.
Still negotiates the concrete commitment and date for the real deliverable.
Notes the contract itself is api-event-architect's; this coordinates delivery.

cases.prompt: Our ask is low on the other team's backlog but they're nice people, so let's just assume they'll get to it in time.

assertions: Refuses to assume goodwill delivers a deprioritized ask: they're not hostile, you're just fourth on their list.
Aligns incentives, offers a trade, or escalates to shared management for an explicit prioritization decision (a Stop Condition if they can't commit).
Sets a fallback in case the ask stays deprioritized.
Does not record an uncommitted dependency as handled.

cases.prompt: Design the API contract at the boundary between our services: routes, the envelope, versioning, and rate limits.

assertions: Does not invoke cross-team-dependency-negotiator: designing the interface contract (routes/envelope/versioning) is api-event-architect.
This skill negotiates who delivers the dependency and when, not the contract's shape.
